findings.title: Direct no-write unit-test loop omits three flags that are only enforced by the coverage validator
findings.evidence: The per-profile loop in test "CP00-131 verdict, approval, validation, and harness rows stay no-write and reference-only" asserts ~19 no-write flags but does not directly assert executes_analytics_query, executes_export_download, or unauthorized_count_exposed. All three are still enforced via validatePermissionKernelCp131Coverage() (asserted through coverage.valid in the catalog test) and the per-profile loop in scripts/validate-rp02-permission-kernel-contract.mjs.
findings.recommendation: Optional symmetry only: add the three missing assertions to the direct test. Non-blocking, since the values are hard-set to false in freezeNoWriteResult and enforced by both the coverage validator and the contract validation script.

parsed_review.recommendation: Approve CP00-131 for closeout. No P0/P1/P2 findings; the single P3 is informational and already compensated by existing coverage.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope and units: CP131_UNITS defines exactly 10 frozen rows mapped to RP02.P08.M06.S08-S17 (indices 8-17), covering all 10 required deliverables — audit summary receipt, no-real-data receipt, Claude dependency marker, human approval marker, PASS, PASS_WITH_FINDINGS, BLOCK, evidence template, validation command check, and harness boundary note. Domain counts (evidence_receipt 3 / review_dependency 1 / verdict 6) and deliverable counts (hermes_evidence 3 / claude_review 1 / implementation 6) match the manifest, coverage validator, tests, and contract script. Inheritance: the module imports CP130's contract/boundary/case functions and asserts inherited_result_count===10, permission_summary_reference_only, command_result_no_raw_output, and CP130's handoff to CP00-131 — self-guarding the inheritance claim. Synthetic/no-write: every row carries synthetic_only/no_real_data true and product_state_effect 'none'; freezeNoWriteResult hard-sets all 19 no-write flags false; CP131_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION mirrors the contract JSON's no_write_attestation, and the validator/script verify both source and contract sides are false. Reference-only behaviors verified: audit/no-real-data receipts emit no ledger entry, persist nothing, and render no hidden template fields; Claude dependency marker sets executes_claude_review false with exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required true; human approval marker sets grants_human_approval/marks_production_ready false; verdict semantics are metadata-only with block_prevents_production_ready and pass_with_findings_requires_adjudication; evidence template, validation command check (command_executed_by_runtime/raw_output_disclosed false), and harness boundary note (harness_runtime_invoked/writes_hermes_runtime false) are all inert. The 10 declared hidden_source_fields are never populated in any result; hidden_field_names_exposed is false. No runtime UI/API routes, policy mutation, audit/product/database writes, idempotency persistence, locks, retries, rollback, compensation, export/share, AI/analytics, LDIP, or HRX split are introduced — replay_command and Hermes command_anchors are inert reference strings. Functions are pure (no fs/network/db/process side effects); unknown case IDs fail closed. Contract schema bumped v0.24→v0.25, current_pack advanced to CP00-131 with the updated production_ready flag, CP130's section preserved, and handoff fixed to CP00-132 / RP02.P08.M06.S18 consistently across contract, code, README, and tests.

raw_result.result:
## CP00-131 Pack-Level Claude Review

I reviewed the staged diff for the RP02 Permission Kernel Risk A Hermes evidence synthetic fixture verdict boundary pack. The pack is metadata-only, reference-only, and no-write across all 10 planned units, inherits CP130 correctly, and is fully self-validated. No blocking issues.

```json

  "overall_verdict": "PASS",
  "finding_counts": { "p0": 0, "p1": 0, "p2": 0, "p3": 1 },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "P3",
      "title": "Direct no-write unit-test loop omits three flags that are only enforced by the coverage validator",
      "file": "packages/authz/test/authz.test.js",
      "evidence": "The per-profile loop in test \"CP00-131 verdict, approval, validation, and harness rows stay no-write and reference-only\" asserts ~19 no-write flags but does not directly assert executes_analytics_query, executes_export_download, or unauthorized_count_exposed. All three are still enforced via validatePermissionKernelCp131Coverage() (asserted through coverage.valid in the catalog test) and the per-profile loop in scripts/validate-rp02-permission-kernel-contract.mjs.",
      "recommendation": "Optional symmetry only: add the three missing assertions to the direct test. Non-blocking, since the values are hard-set to false in freezeNoWriteResult and enforced by both the coverage validator and the contract validation script."
    }
  ],
  "blocks_pack_closeout": false,
  "recommendation": "Approve CP00-131 for closeout. No P0/P1/P2 findings; the single P3 is informational and already compensated by existing coverage.",
  "rationale": "Scope and units: CP131_UNITS defines exactly 10 frozen rows mapped to RP02.P08.M06.S08-S17 (indices 8-17), covering all 10 required deliverables — audit summary receipt, no-real-data receipt, Claude dependency marker, human approval marker, PASS, PASS_WITH_FINDINGS, BLOCK, evidence template, validation command check, and harness boundary note. Domain counts (evidence_receipt 3 / review_dependency 1 / verdict 6) and deliverable counts (hermes_evidence 3 / claude_review 1 / implementation 6) match the manifest, coverage validator, tests, and contract script. Inheritance: the module imports CP130's contract/boundary/case functions and asserts inherited_result_count===10, permission_summary_reference_only, command_result_no_raw_output, and CP130's handoff to CP00-131 — self-guarding the inheritance claim. Synthetic/no-write: every row carries synthetic_only/no_real_data true and product_state_effect 'none'; freezeNoWriteResult hard-sets all 19 no-write flags false; CP131_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION mirrors the contract JSON's no_write_attestation, and the validator/script verify both source and contract sides are false. Reference-only behaviors verified: audit/no-real-data receipts emit no ledger entry, persist nothing, and render no hidden template fields; Claude dependency marker sets executes_claude_review false with exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required true; human approval marker sets grants_human_approval/marks_production_ready false; verdict semantics are metadata-only with block_prevents_production_ready and pass_with_findings_requires_adjudication; evidence template, validation command check (command_executed_by_runtime/raw_output_disclosed false), and harness boundary note (harness_runtime_invoked/writes_hermes_runtime false) are all inert. The 10 declared hidden_source_fields are never populated in any result; hidden_field_names_exposed is false. No runtime UI/API routes, policy mutation, audit/product/database writes, idempotency persistence, locks, retries, rollback, compensation, export/share, AI/analytics, LDIP, or HRX split are introduced — replay_command and Hermes command_anchors are inert reference strings. Functions are pure (no fs/network/db/process side effects); unknown case IDs fail closed. Contract schema bumped v0.24→v0.25, current_pack advanced to CP00-131 with the updated production_ready flag, CP130's section preserved, and handoff fixed to CP00-132 / RP02.P08.M06.S18 consistently across contract, code, README, and tests."
}
```

I did not run any commands or modify any files; this was a read-only review of the staged diff.
